12.reason: The return value of the recursive call is ignored; the function does not propagate the result back, so it always returns the initial sum when op==0.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is ignored; the function does not propagate the result back, so it always returns the initial sum when op==0.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is ignored; the updated sum is not captured, so the function always returns the initial sum when op==0.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Recursive call return value is ignored; should be assigned or returned to propagate the chain length.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  missing return; recursive result not propagated
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is ignored, so the accumulated length from deeper recursion levels is lost. It should be `return f(i, sum);` or the result should be captured and returned.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is ignored, so the updated sum from deeper recursion levels is lost. It should be sum = f(i, sum); or return f(i, sum); depending on logic.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call's return value is ignored, so the accumulated sum from deeper recursion is lost. This causes the function to return an incorrect (usually smaller) count.]

19.reason: [The function does not return a value in all control paths. When op==1, the function ends without returning anything, leading to undefined behavior.]
